label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the students performed in the experiment. The answer choices are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall what the study found. The passage says that Dr. Dolcos and her colleague conducted an experiment with 143 undergrads. Half wrote advice in first-person, the others in second-person. The results were that those who used second-person completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Also, when it came to exercising more, the second-person group again reported a more positive attitude and planned to exercise more. So looking at the options: A) First-person had better attitude. But the study says the opposite, that second-person had better attitude. So A is wrong. B) Those who talked to themselves completed most puzzles. But the experiment had them writing, not talking out loud. The passage mentions that the researchers asked them to write down advice. So B is not correct because it mentions talking, which wasn't the method used in the experiment. The study compared first vs second person in written self-talk, not spoken. C) Those who used second-person writing were better. The passage says they completed more puzzles and had more positive attitude. So C matches
